research_questions.title: Sentence-Level ESG Consistency
research_questions.question: How consistent are large language models in extracting ESG-relevant sentences from the same disclosure documents?

research_questions.title: Aspect–Sentiment–Tone Distributions
research_questions.question: How do sentiment and tone distributions vary across ESG aspect categories at the sentence level?

research_questions.title: Tone Bias and Imbalance
research_questions.question: Do LLMs exhibit systematic tone bias (e.g., overuse of commitments versus outcomes) across ESG categories?

research_questions.title: Grounding and Hallucination
research_questions.question: To what extent are extracted ESG sentences grounded in the original source documents?

research_questions.title: Explainable Aspect Clustering
research_questions.question: Can manual, ontology-driven aspect clustering improve interpretability without sacrificing sentence-level traceability?

objectives.text: Evaluate inter-model consistency in ESG sentence extraction and labeling
Analyze sentence-level sentiment and tone distributions across ESG aspects
Detect and quantify tone imbalance and annotation bias
Ensure grounding fidelity of ESG sentences to source documents
Develop auditable, ontology-driven aspect clustering for ESG narratives

methodology.label: Sentence Extraction
methodology.description: Extract ESG-relevant sentences from disclosure documents using multiple LLMs.

methodology.label: Ontology Normalization
methodology.description: Normalize extracted sentences into predefined ESG aspect, sentiment, and tone ontologies.

methodology.label: Distribution Analysis
methodology.description: Compute global and conditional distributions of aspects, sentiment, and tone.

methodology.label: Minimum-Tone Analysis
methodology.description: Identify sparsity and imbalance using minimum-tone metrics across aspect–sentiment groups.

methodology.label: Grounding Audit
methodology.description: Verify that extracted ESG sentences are present in the original source text.

methodology.label: Model Comparison
methodology.description: Compare sentence coverage and labeling agreement across multiple LLMs.

methodology.label: Manual Aspect Clustering
methodology.description: Cluster fine-grained aspects into auditable, human-defined thematic groups.

hypotheses.text: Different LLMs produce significantly different ESG sentence coverage on identical documents
Certain ESG aspects are systematically associated with dominant tones such as commitments over outcomes
Grounding audits reveal non-trivial rates of hallucinated or weakly grounded ESG sentences
Manual ontology-driven aspect clustering improves interpretability of ESG narratives without reducing traceability
